title: Dorling Kindersley

'''Dorling Kindersley''' (also known as '''DK''' or '''DK Publishing''') is an international publishing company specializing in reference [[Wikipedia:book|books]] for adults and children.

DK was founded as a book-packaging company by [[Wikipedia:Peter Kindersley|Peter Kindersley]] and [[Wikipedia:Christopher Dorling|Christopher Dorling]] in [[Wikipedia:London|London]] in [[1974]], and in [[1982]] moved into publishing. The first book published under the DK name was a ''First Aid Manual'' for the British voluntary medical services; this book established the company's distinctive visual style of copiously illustrated text on a glossy white background. DK Inc. began publishing in the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]] in [[1991]].<ref name="Timeline" />

The DK ''[[Star Wars]]'' contract was signed in [[1997]], and the company had published ''Star Wars'' books ever since.<ref name="Timeline" />

In [[2000]], DK was acquired by the Pearson PLC media company, which also owns the [[Penguin Books]] label; in the same year, Kindersley retired and was replaced as Chief Executive by [[Wikipedia:Anthony Forbes Watson|Anthony Forbes Watson]].<ref name="Timeline" />

In Germany the ''Star Wars'' books from Dorling Kindersley are published by VGS, a brand of the [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] publisher Egmont Ehapa Verlag, even though DK has a German subsidiary.

DK Publishing is one of the creators of Star Wars Reads Day, an annual celebration held in bookstores and libraries.<ref name="readsday">{{WebCite|url=http://wpri.com/2014/10/06/author-returns-for-star-wars-reads-day|text=Author returns for 'Star Wars Reads Day'|author=Caligiuri, Courtney|date=2014-10-06|archivedate=20141008041848}}</ref>
